source.title: Differential effects on the laser evoked potential of selectively attending to pain localisation versus pain unpleasantness.

confidence_rationale: EEG study with 21 healthy subjects directly comparing neural responses to localisation vs unpleasantness attention tasks during laser-evoked pain. The finding that N300-T7 (contralateral temporal/S2) is selectively enhanced during localisation but not unpleasantness directly confirms that the lateral pain system (S1/S2 pathway, z_harm_s analog) is preferentially engaged by sensory-discriminative processing. Co-authored by Treede, a leading figure in nociceptive electrophysiology. Modest sample size and some uncertainty about precise source localisation limit confidence slightly.
failure_signatures: N300-T7 amplitude equivalent for localisation and unpleasantness tasks (no lateral system selectivity)
Medial system indices (FCz, P450) shown to be differentially modulated by localisation task
Both tasks shown to enhance lateral and medial components equally

mapping.source_claim_statement: N300-T7 (reflecting S2/posterior insula activity) is significantly greater during attention to pain location (sensory-discriminative processing) than during attention to pain unpleasantness. This confirms a role for the lateral pain system in localisation, distinct from the medial system's role in affective-motivational processing.
mapping.ree_translation: z_harm_s is the latent variable downstream of the lateral pain system (A-delta/VPL/S1-S2 projection). Bentley et al. show that attending to WHERE pain is (the task z_harm_s must support -- proximity encoding for action guidance) selectively upregulates this pathway. Attending to HOW UNPLEASANT it is (the task z_harm_a supports -- motivational weighting for drive escalation) does not additionally recruit S2. This is the functional double dissociation SD-011 requires: z_harm_s for spatial-discriminative encoding; z_harm_a for affective-motivational encoding.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The study uses attention manipulation, not pathway lesion, so both systems are active in both conditions -- the dissociation is in the degree of engagement, not absolute separation. The N300-T7 source estimate (S2/posterior insula) is from scalp EEG with limited spatial resolution. The study does not directly probe medial thalamic or ACC involvement, which would be the z_harm_a side of the dissociation.
mapping.source_context: Written in the context of understanding how top-down attention modulates distinct pain processing streams. Rolf-Detlef Treede is one of the leading researchers in laser evoked potentials and has extensively characterised the lateral vs medial pain system distinction.

confidence_components.notes: Good journal, strong experimental design with matched attention tasks, well-cited. Mapping fidelity good: the selectivity of N300-T7 for localisation maps cleanly onto z_harm_s being the discriminative stream. Low transfer risk: this is human EEG of the exact pathway SD-011 formalises.
